passages.text: Global warming refers to the long-term rise in the average temperature of the Earth's climate system. This phenomenon is primarily caused by the increasing concentrations of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere, resulting from human activities such as the burning of fossil fuels and deforestation. These gases, including carbon dioxide and methane, trap heat from the sun, leading to a gradual increase in global temperatures. The consequences of global warming are far-reaching and include melting polar ice caps, rising sea levels, and more frequent extreme weather events. Addressing this issue requires a concerted global effort to reduce greenhouse gas emissions and transition to renewable energy sources.

The Renaissance was a period of intense cultural, artistic, and intellectual activity that began in Italy in the 14th century and later spread throughout Europe. It marked the transition from the Middle Ages to the modern era, characterized by a revival of interest in the classical learning of ancient Greece and Rome. During this time, there was a shift towards humanism, an intellectual movement that emphasized the value and agency of human beings. Artists such as Michelangelo and Leonardo da Vinci created masterpieces that celebrated the human form and the natural world. Furthermore, the invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg facilitated the spread of ideas, contributing to the rise of literacy and the Protestant Reformation.

passages.title: Cloze Test 17-21
passages.text: Despite the complexity of the task, the engineers managed to complete the bridge ahead of schedule.

passages.title: Cloze Test 22-26
passages.text:
Dr. Aris: The results of the bone density tests are quite concerning. 
Patient: Does that mean I am at a high risk for fractures? 
Dr. Aris: ____ 
Patient: I see. What steps can I take to improve my condition?

questions.question: The rapid ____ of urban areas often leads to significant environmental degradation if sustainable planning measures are not strictly enforced.

options.A: deterioration
options.B: proliferation
options.C: mitigation
options.D: diminution
options.E: rectification

questions.question: In the late 19th century, many scientists remained ____ about the germ theory of disease, clinging instead to the long-held belief in miasma.

options.A: indispensable
options.B: skeptical
options.C: ambivalent
options.D: vulnerable
options.E: persistent

questions.question: The research findings clearly ____ that regular physical activity significantly reduces the risk of chronic cardiovascular conditions.

options.A: undermine
options.B: negate
options.C: substantiate
options.D: contradict
options.E: forestall

questions.question: Although the initial results of the clinical trial were promising, ____.

options.A: the team decided to publish the findings in a prestigious journal
options.B: subsequent phases failed to produce statistically significant improvements
options.C: most participants reported a marked decrease in their symptoms
options.D: it was widely accepted as a breakthrough in genetic engineering
options.E: the researchers were awarded a substantial grant for further study

questions.question: ____, the government has decided to implement stricter regulations on carbon emissions.

options.A: In spite of the economic benefits of industrial growth
options.B: Because the air quality has shown a steady improvement recently
options.C: Unless the international community reaches a consensus on climate change
options.D: Driven by the urgent need to address the escalating environmental crisis
options.E: Regardless of the fact that renewable energy sources are becoming cheaper

questions.question: The Silk Road served as a vital conduit for trade between the East and West, ____.

options.A: whereby it was primarily used for local exchange of agricultural goods
options.B: yet it had very little impact on the cultural development of Europe
options.C: facilitating the exchange of not only goods but also ideas and technologies
options.D: nevertheless it remained largely unknown until the modern era
options.E: so that the maritime routes were completely abandoned by merchants

questions.question: Psychologists suggest that early childhood experiences are crucial ____.

options.A: as they lay the foundation for an individual's emotional and social development
options.B: even though most people forget their childhood memories by adulthood
options.C: while the genetic factors are the sole determinants of human behavior
options.D: so as to ensure that all children grow up to be successful professionals
options.E: despite the fact that personality traits are mostly fixed at birth

questions.question: Humanity has long been fascinated by the possibility of extraterrestrial life. For decades, astronomers (5)____ the cosmos for signals from distant civilizations.

options.A: have been scanning
options.B: had scanned
options.C: will have scanned
options.D: would scan
options.E: are being scanned

questions.question: While no definitive evidence has been found yet, the discovery of thousands of exoplanets (6)____ our understanding of the universe's potential for life.

options.A: has revolutionized
options.B: was revolutionizing
options.C: revolves
options.D: had revolutionized
options.E: is revolutionized

questions.question: Many of these planets are located within the 'habitable zone,' (7)____ liquid water could theoretically exist.

options.A: which
options.B: where
options.C: whose
options.D: when
options.E: that

questions.question: (8)____ the vast distances between stars, sending a physical probe to these worlds remains a daunting challenge.

options.A: In contrast to
options.B: Instead of
options.C: Due to
options.D: Regardless of
options.E: In addition to

questions.question: Nevertheless, advancements in propulsion technology (9)____ make interstellar travel a reality in the distant future.

options.A: might
options.B: must
options.C: should
options.D: ought to
options.E: would have to

questions.question: Sanayi Devrimi, sadece üretim yöntemlerini değiştirmekle kalmamış, aynı zamanda sosyal yapıyı ve aile dinamiklerini de derinden etkilemiştir.

options.A: The Industrial Revolution changed production methods and deeply affected social structure and family dynamics.
options.B: Not only did the Industrial Revolution change production methods, but it also deeply affected the social structure and family dynamics.
options.C: The Industrial Revolution affected the social structure and family dynamics as much as it changed production methods.
options.D: While the Industrial Revolution was changing production methods, it also had a deep impact on social structure and family dynamics.
options.E: Production methods were changed by the Industrial Revolution, which also deeply affected the social structure and family dynamics.

questions.question: Kuantum fiziği, atom altı parçacıkların davranışlarını açıklamaya çalışan ve geleneksel fizik kurallarına meydan okuyan bir bilim dalıdır.

options.A: Quantum physics is a branch of science that tries to explain the behavior of subatomic particles and challenges traditional rules of physics.
options.B: A branch of science that challenges traditional rules of physics and explains the behavior of subatomic particles is quantum physics.
options.C: Quantum physics, which challenges traditional physical rules, is a science explaining how subatomic particles behave.
options.D: The behavior of subatomic particles is explained by quantum physics, which challenges the traditional rules of physics.
options.E: Traditional rules of physics are challenged by quantum physics, a science that explains the behavior of subatomic particles.

questions.question: Modern medicine has progressed to the point where many diseases that were once fatal can now be managed or even cured.

options.A: Modern tıp, bir zamanlar ölümcül olan birçok hastalığın artık yönetilebileceği veya hatta tedavi edilebileceği noktaya ilerlemiştir.
options.B: Modern tıp ilerlediği için, bir zamanlar ölümcül olan pek çok hastalık artık tedavi edilebiliyor veya yönetilebiliyor.
options.C: Bir zamanlar ölümcül olan hastalıkların yönetilmesi veya tedavi edilmesi, modern tıbbın geldiği noktadır.
options.D: Modern tıptaki ilerlemeler sayesinde, eskiden ölümcül olan hastalıklar artık kolayca tedavi edilmektedir.
options.E: Ölümcül hastalıkların yönetilmesi ve tedavi edilmesi modern tıbbın en büyük başarısıdır.

questions.question: The rapid depletion of natural resources forces governments to seek sustainable energy alternatives as soon as possible.

options.A: Doğal kaynakların hızla tükenmesi, hükümetleri bir an önce sürdürülebilir enerji alternatifleri aramaya zorluyor.
options.B: Hükümetler, doğal kaynaklar hızla tükendiği için sürdürülebilir enerji alternatiflerini mümkün olan en kısa sürede bulmalıdır.
options.C: Sürdürülebilir enerji alternatifleri arayan hükümetler, doğal kaynakların tükenmesini engellemeye çalışmaktadır.
options.D: Doğal kaynaklar hızla tükendiğinden, hükümetlerin sürdürülebilir enerji alternatifleri araması zorunlu hale gelmiştir.
options.E: Hükümetlerin bir an önce sürdürülebilir enerji alternatifleri araması, doğal kaynakların hızlı tükenmesiyle ilgilidir.

questions.question: Boşluk 17 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The engineers completed the bridge early even though the task was complicated.
options.B: The task was so complex that the engineers could not complete the bridge on time.
options.C: Because the task was complex, the engineers had to delay the completion of the bridge.
options.D: The engineers completed the bridge ahead of schedule because the task was quite simple.
options.E: Although the bridge was completed early, the task was not as complex as expected.

questions.question: Boşluk 18 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: The protests started just before the new law was passed.
options.B: As soon as the new law was passed, protests broke out throughout the country.
options.C: The new law was passed after the protests had already started.
options.D: The country remained peaceful even after the new law was passed.
options.E: The protests caused the government to pass the new law immediately.

questions.question: Boşluk 19 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Most people think the Maya civilization collapsed solely because of environmental issues.
options.B: Internal warfare is considered the only reason for the decline of the Maya civilization.
options.C: Environmental factors and internal warfare are commonly thought to have caused the decline of the Maya civilization.
options.D: The decline of the Maya civilization led to environmental problems and internal warfare.
options.E: It is a fact that the Maya civilization declined because of environmental factors and internal warfare.

questions.question: Boşluk 20 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Practicing a new language does not always guarantee fluency.
options.B: Your fluency in a new language increases in proportion to how much you practice it.
options.C: You should practice a new language if you want to become fluent.
options.D: Fluency in a new language is achieved only through constant practice.
options.E: The less you practice a new language, the less likely you are to become fluent.

questions.question: Boşluk 21 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: What do you think is the biggest challenge for these countries?
options.B: It's a broad topic, though. Are you planning to focus on a particular region?
options.C: Renewable energy is indeed expensive for many developing nations.
options.D: Most studies on this topic are already quite comprehensive.
options.E: Are you suggesting that technology transfer is the only solution?

questions.question: Boşluk 22 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Not necessarily, but we should definitely start a treatment plan.
options.B: Yes, your bone density is perfectly normal for your age.
options.C: Fractures are common among the elderly, so don't worry.
options.D: We need to perform more tests to be absolutely sure.
options.E: The risk is minimal, so no immediate action is required.

questions.question: Boşluk 23 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: What was the most difficult project you ever managed?
options.B: How did you handle conflicts within such a diverse team?
options.C: Did you use any specific software for project management?
options.D: Why did you decide to leave your previous company?
options.E: What did you learn about leadership from that experience?

questions.question: Boşluk 24 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: I think climate change is the main reason for this decline.
options.B: Bees are not the only insects that pollinate crops.
options.C: We should encourage people to keep bees in their backyards.
options.D: The government needs to ban all types of pesticides immediately.
options.E: Do you think the report is based on reliable data?

questions.question: Boşluk 25 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: These systems are already being integrated into various industries, from healthcare to finance.
options.B: Most AI researchers agree that we are still far from achieving true general intelligence.
options.C: The history of AI is marked by periods of great excitement followed by 'AI winters.'
options.D: Machine learning is a subset of AI that focuses on training algorithms to learn from data.
options.E: AI applications in the creative arts have sparked a debate about the nature of authorship.

questions.question: Boşluk 26 için en uygun seçeneği bulun.

options.A: Museums play a key role in the preservation of historical artifacts.
options.B: It encompasses the entirety of a society's creative expressions and knowledge.
options.C: Many countries have established laws to protect their national monuments.
options.D: Tourism can sometimes have a negative impact on local cultural traditions.
options.E: The UNESCO World Heritage list includes many sites of outstanding universal value.

questions.question: The human brain is an incredibly complex organ, responsible for controlling every aspect of our physiological and cognitive functions. It consists of billions of neurons that communicate with each other through intricate networks. ____. For instance, the prefrontal cortex is involved in high-level decision-making, while the hippocampus is crucial for memory formation.

options.A: Neuroscientists are still trying to map all the connections in the human brain.
options.B: Different regions of the brain are specialized for specific tasks and functions.
options.C: Brain plasticity allows the organ to adapt and reorganize itself throughout life.
options.D: Mental health disorders are often linked to chemical imbalances in the brain.
options.E: Advances in neuroimaging have allowed us to observe the brain in action.

questions.question: Urbanization is a global phenomenon characterized by the migration of people from rural areas to cities in search of better opportunities. This trend has led to the growth of megacities with populations exceeding ten million. ____. These include overcrowding, inadequate housing, and increased pressure on public services like transportation and healthcare.

options.A: Cities are often seen as hubs of innovation and economic growth.
options.B: Despite these challenges, many people continue to move to urban centers.
options.C: However, this rapid growth brings about a host of social and infrastructure issues.
options.D: Urban planning is essential for creating sustainable and livable cities.
options.E: The environmental impact of large cities is a major concern for policymakers.

questions.question: Hardly had the scientist published his findings when they were challenged by his peers.

options.A: The scientist's findings were challenged by his peers immediately after they were published.
options.B: The scientist published his findings even though he knew they would be challenged.
options.C: His peers challenged the findings before the scientist had a chance to publish them.
options.D: The scientist decided not to publish his findings because he feared they would be challenged.
options.E: It took a long time for the scientist's peers to challenge his published findings.

questions.question: It is possible that the flight was canceled because of the heavy snowstorm.

options.A: The flight was definitely canceled due to the heavy snowstorm.
options.B: The heavy snowstorm might have caused the cancellation of the flight.
options.C: The flight would have been canceled if there had been a heavy snowstorm.
options.D: The heavy snowstorm did not prevent the flight from taking off.
options.E: The flight was canceled despite the fact that there was no snowstorm.

questions.question: She wouldn't have failed the exam if she had studied more consistently throughout the semester.

options.A: She failed the exam because she did not study consistently during the semester.
options.B: Even though she studied consistently, she still failed the exam.
options.C: She passed the exam because she studied hard at the end of the semester.
options.D: She would fail the exam even if she studied more consistently.
options.E: Studying more consistently did not help her pass the exam.

questions.question: (I) The Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system, located off the coast of Australia. (II) It is home to thousands of species of marine life, including colorful fish, turtles, and sharks. (III) Climate change and rising ocean temperatures are posing a major threat to its survival. (IV) Australia's economy relies heavily on the tourism industry centered around the reef. (V) Divers from all over the world visit the reef to experience its unique beauty.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Leonardo da Vinci was a true Renaissance man, excelling in both art and science. (II) His most famous work, the Mona Lisa, is known for its enigmatic smile. (III) Many artists of the Renaissance period were supported by wealthy patrons like the Medici family. (IV) Besides painting, Leonardo made significant contributions to anatomy, engineering, and optics. (V) His notebooks contain thousands of sketches and observations that were far ahead of his time.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: (I) Standardized testing is often used as a measure of student achievement and school accountability. (II) Proponents argue that it provides an objective way to compare student performance across different regions. (III) However, critics claim that it leads to a 'teaching to the test' culture, narrowing the curriculum. (IV) Some schools have started to implement project-based learning as an alternative to traditional methods. (V) Furthermore, standardized tests may not accurately reflect a student's true potential or creativity.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

questions.question: According to the passage, what is the primary cause of global warming?

options.A: The depletion of the ozone layer by CFCs.
options.B: Natural fluctuations in the Earth's climate.
options.C: The accumulation of greenhouse gases in the atmosphere.
options.D: The increase in solar radiation reaching the Earth's surface.
options.E: The melting of polar ice caps and rising sea levels.

questions.question: What does the passage mention as a consequence of global warming?

options.A: A decrease in the frequency of extreme weather events.
options.B: A reduction in the concentration of methane in the atmosphere.
options.C: The expansion of polar ice caps due to cooler temperatures.
options.D: An increase in sea levels resulting from melting ice.
options.E: The complete disappearance of the greenhouse effect.

questions.question: What is suggested as a solution to global warming in the passage?

options.A: Increasing the use of fossil fuels to stimulate the economy.
options.B: Planting more trees to absorb carbon dioxide from the air.
options.C: Moving away from renewable energy and back to traditional sources.
options.D: Reducing greenhouse gas emissions and adopting renewable energy.
options.E: Ignoring the issue as it is a natural part of Earth's climate.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a function of the Renaissance?

options.A: A revival of interest in the classical learning of Greece and Rome.
options.B: The development of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg.
options.C: A shift towards a more secular and human-centered worldview.
options.D: The exploration of new lands and the expansion of trade routes.
options.E: The immediate end of all religious influence on art and culture.

questions.question: How did the printing press contribute to the Renaissance, according to the passage?

options.A: It led to a decrease in the production of books and manuscripts.
options.B: It helped to spread ideas and increased literacy rates among the public.
options.C: It was used exclusively for printing religious texts in Latin.
options.D: It had no significant impact on the intellectual life of the period.
options.E: It made it more difficult for people to access classical Greek and Roman texts.

questions.question: The term 'humanism' in the passage is associated with:

options.A: A focus on the divine and the afterlife rather than earthly life.
options.B: An intellectual movement emphasizing the importance of human beings.
options.C: The strict adherence to traditional medieval religious doctrines.
options.D: A rejection of all artistic and cultural achievements of the past.
options.E: The belief that humans have no control over their own lives.
